summary: The package root export face is holed: `kind()`s own hook and facts types are unreachable from it, and `globValid` ‚Äî 1 of 24 predicate constructors ‚Äî never re-exports

new.description: New: the export face gets a home that holds it MECHANICALLY, not a hand-list that re-rots on the next added type ‚Äî the field report's own argument (an exported signature naming an unexported type just moves the trap one level down). Load `typescript` (already a devDependency) and walk the emitted `dist/src/index.d.ts`, which exists at test time (`pnpm --dir sdk test` runs `tsc -p` before `node --test`): assert every type a root-exported declaration names is itself root-exported. Precedent for asserting over the root's export surface exists at `sdk/test/emit.test.ts:623` (`import * as sdk`, asserting removed child-kind constructors are `undefined`) ‚Äî that is a negative one-off inside an emit test, not an export-surface home; name the consideration in the commit body per engineering.md. **The seam half is out of scope and excluded by the walk**: `Declarations`/`PayloadMember` and the generated row family they reach are routed to the `(seam-rows-public-face)` fork ‚Äî the walk excludes `src/generated/**` and says so, never silently. If the mechanical walk proves infeasible in one tick, the honest fallback is an enumerated type-import test PLUS a stated gap in the commit body ‚Äî never a silent hand-list.

edit.description: The root export face (the `./kind.js` type block at 54-71, the `./contract.js` block at 26-50, the `./emit.js` block at 90). Six types are named by root-exported signatures yet never re-export ‚Äî verified on disk, the closure the field report asked for rather than the pair it reported: `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberValue` (kind.ts:384), named by `KindDefinition.render` (226), `KindOptions.render` (276) and `EmbeddedMemberValue.render` (340); `EdgeTargetFacts` (367) and `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberCollectionEntry` (353), named by that type's `targets` (399) and `collections` (392); `KindOptions` (275), the second parameter of the root-exported `kind()` (286); `Template` (120), named by the root-exported `KindFacts.templates` (163); `SettingsResidue` (emit.ts:601), named by the root-exported `EmitResult.settings`. Plus one VALUE hole in the same face: `globValid` (contract.ts:116) is the only one of the 24 predicate constructors absent from the root block ‚Äî used solely by `builtins.ts` (503/595), so an author cannot spell `clause(globValid("paths"), ‚Ä¶)` over their own kind without deep-importing. The predicate vocabulary is closed and the enum in code is the authority (`contract.md`, "clause"); an author-facing vocabulary 23/24 wide silently disagrees with it.

Line 37 deep-imports `ResolvedEmbeddedMemberValue` from `../src/kind.js` while line 36 imports the root ‚Äî temper's own test suite reproduces the exact consumer trap the field report names, which is the cheapest available proof the hole is real and not theoretical. Flip that import to `../src/index.js` (the render-hook fixture at 1164 is its consumer). Behavior unchanged; the existing tests are the coverage.

tests.asserts: every type a root-exported declaration names is itself root-exported (walked mechanically over the emitted `index.d.ts`, the seam's generated row family excluded), and `globValid` is reachable from the root beside its 23 sibling predicate constructors
the render-hook fixture types its parameter from the package root, no deep import into `src/kind.js`

acceptance: A consumer types a `render` hook parameter, a `kind()` options bag, a nesting `Template`, and a `globValid` clause off `@dtmd/temper` alone ‚Äî no deep import into dist internals (pipeline.md, "The SDK": the authoring surface is typed at the keystroke, and a type the author cannot import defeats that). The closure is held by a test, not a hand-list. `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 centercode field report (filed 9adefde). Gap re-verified on disk at 420da04, unmoved since its `observed at 58efe11`. Scoped to the VERIFIED closure, not the reported pair: the report named 2 types, the walk found 6 plus the `globValid` value hole. The seam half ‚Äî `Declarations`/`PayloadMember` and the generated rows, root-exported yet spec-declared internal ‚Äî is the `(seam-rows-public-face)` fork, not this entry's to settle. scoped at 420da04.

summary: The built-in adoption: `skill` templates its bundled reference documents at the directory's markdown, and `supporting-doc` ships as their prose-only kind

edit.description: `skill` (142-158) declares no template. Give it its bundled reference documents as one file-child layer ‚Äî `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16). New `supporting-doc` kind beside it: fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename, nested-file locus. Its default contract ships **empty** per the standing bar ("Default contracts"); its one clause is SUPPORTING-DOC-REACH-CLAUSE's to add once an incidence clause outside a requirement is admissible, never this entry's to invent. The kind ships without joining the surface enumeration, the `requirement` precedent. Rider (open-questions): the doc-comment cites at 565/611 name two deleted `packages/{rule,memory}.anthropic/PACKAGE.md` files ‚Äî discharge them while here.

The engine-side reader mirror of the shipped kinds (the `CustomKind::new` calls and `definitions`): `skill`'s row grows its template, and `supporting-doc` enters as the child kind a discovered document is read as ‚Äî 91c288c landed the `CustomKind::nested_file` constructor (src/kind.rs:600) this row is the first built-in caller of. Rider (open-questions): the narration cites here are dated @07-07 / @07-10 or undated (85, 194/222, 63/106) ‚Äî every fact still holds; re-verify against the live docs and bump the dates while here.

The embedded built-in lock re-derives once `skill`'s row carries a template and `supporting-doc` has a row ‚Äî the first built-in to declare either (91c288c left this file byte-identical because no built-in declared the locus; that changes here).
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the two grown rows.
`skill`'s declaration row carries its reference-document template, and `supporting-doc`'s row carries its nested-file locus and empty field schema.

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `skill`'s kind row grows its `templates` column (child kind `supporting-doc`, the markdown path pattern), and `supporting-doc` enters as a new kind row at the nested-file locus.

tests.asserts: `skill` declares one file-child layer of `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, and `supporting-doc` is fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically from the SDK with the two grown rows
a real skill directory's markdown document is discovered as that skill's `supporting-doc` child

acceptance: A skill's bundled reference documents are nested file members of the same skill, typed `supporting-doc` (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"), their path the skill template's declared fact ‚Äî nesting-is-model-containment / locus-is-serialization stops being an unbacked claim. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 3 ‚Äî the built-in adoption; 0027 says it re-enters buildable, and it does, minus the clause. The template claims the honest markdown subset: supporting files of other types stay unmodeled and named as such (0027's second Rejected bullet), the `settings.json` partial-governance posture. Discharges the `supportingDocs()` factory asymmetry in open-questions. Gate re-tested at b745415: NESTED-FILE-DISCOVERY unshipped. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: The fixture-harness wiring builder belongs in `tests/common`, not copy-pasted per suite: three copies of tmpdir ‚Üí `.temper` ‚Üí `harness.ts` ‚Üí `vendor_sdk` collapse into one home

edit.description: The declared one home for test scaffolding ‚Äî its own header (1-4) names "the SDK vendoring used by tests that drive a real `node` subprocess" as the copy-per-suite it exists to end. It carries every ingredient (`tmpdir` 31, `scaffold` 44, `vendor_sdk` 96) and not the builder that composes them into a fixture harness, which is exactly the surface the three copies below each re-derive. Add that builder: a label plus the fixture program text, returning `(harness, into)`. `tests/install.rs`'s `vendor_sdk` calls are a different job ‚Äî the install verb scaffolds its own `harness.ts` under test, rather than wiring an authored fixture program ‚Äî and are out of scope.

`wire_sdk_harness_program` (1223) is the original, and the surface the other two copies' doc comments cite by name as the pattern they follow; `wire_sdk_harness` (1217) binds it to this suite's `HARNESS_PROGRAM`. Retire the local body onto the common home ‚Äî the `HARNESS_PROGRAM` binding stays suite-local, being this suite's fixture rather than scaffolding.

`wire_memberless_harness` (61-71): a verbatim copy of emit.rs's builder ‚Äî tmpdir, `.temper`, write `harness.ts`, `vendor_sdk` ‚Äî whose own doc (58-60) cites `tests/emit.rs`'s `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it follows. Call the common builder with `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM`.

`wire_waypoint_harness` (141, shifted +24 by 91c288c's nested-file-child seam case), the third copy (e76ec85), carrying the same doc cite back to emit.rs's builder. Call the common builder with `WAYPOINT_PROGRAM`.

acceptance: One builder composes tmpdir ‚Üí `.temper` ‚Üí `harness.ts` ‚Üí `vendor_sdk`, in `tests/common`, and no suite re-derives it (engineering.md, "One job, one home": shared fixtures and builders live in one home, never copy-pasted per file). Behavior is unchanged, so the three suites' existing tests are the coverage; `cargo test` green.
notes: Found sweeping 74f4e62..ca4e866: e76ec85 added the third copy, its doc citing emit.rs's `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it followed rather than the home it should have extended ‚Äî the copy-per-suite `tests/common`'s own header says it exists to end. Serialized behind the chain: shares projection_path_seam.rs with the nested-file work, builtin_lock_frozen.rs with SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS. Re-verified at b745415: all three copies live. rescoped at b745415.

summary: `supporting-doc`'s reach clause: a supporting file is invisible unless its host skill references it ‚Äî the by-kind universal binding, each-grain incidence, advisory

edit.description: `supporting-doc`'s default contract ‚Äî the empty exported clause array SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS ships ‚Äî gains its one clause: an incoming-degree bound over the kind's members, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16), the vendor fact that every supporting file is invisible unless SKILL.md references it. **Universal, not existential**: the fact holds of every supporting document, so the spelling is the by-kind binding with each-grain incidence (`contract.md`, "selection"), never a requirement ‚Äî a requirement is the opt-in selector and would make a vendor fact consumer ceremony. **Reach is locus-agnostic**: any resolved edge from the host skill counts, post-0025's one edge noun and one degree semantics ‚Äî a mention included, this clause being the "a contract may" that mention's obligation-free default reserves (`contract.md`, "edge"). Severity **advisory**, per `intent.md` invariant 5: a shipped coverage clause enters advisory, and escalation is the corpus's declared act.
The embedded built-in lock re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains its one clause row.
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the grown clause row.
`supporting-doc`'s default contract carries exactly one clause, at advisory severity, cited and guided ‚Äî the shipped-clause bar (`builtins.md`, "The clauses live in code").

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains one clause row (the incoming-degree reach bound, advisory).

tests.asserts: `supporting-doc`'s default contract is one advisory reach clause, cited to the vendor fact and carrying its guidance
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically with the clause row

acceptance: A supporting document no edge from its host skill reaches is an advisory finding naming the vendor fact (builtins.md, "Default contracts"; the clause is decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated) ‚Äî and reach counts any resolved edge, locus-agnostic. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 ruling's clause itself (25e9317 resolved the fork). The single-tag gate names PREDICATE-SELECTION-ALGEBRA ‚Äî without it an incidence clause outside a requirement is inadmissible, so the clause cannot ship. It ALSO requires SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS: that entry mints the kind and its empty contract, and shares builtins.ts / builtin_lock.toml / builtin_lock_frozen.rs ‚Äî so this is the queue tail, behind both chains. scoped at d572827.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary‚Üînpm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) ‚Äî no signing creds ‚Äî but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead ‚Äî release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at b745415: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml:7-9 still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
